rationale: Both my round-2 items are now soundly reconciled. Item 1: the destructured constants (`const [CMD_PANEL_CREATE, CMD_CODEX_EXEC, CMD_PANEL_VERIFY, CMD_PANEL_TALLY] = ALLOWED_CLI;`, plan L429-430) are present; every call site — R2 `buildCommand(CMD_PANEL_CREATE, slug, spec, target, bead_id?, round?)`, codex `CMD_CODEX_EXEC`, R5 `CMD_PANEL_VERIFY`/`CMD_PANEL_TALLY` — passes an identifier, never a retyped literal; Step 6 pins exactly one `const [ID1,ID2,ID3,ID4] = ALLOWED_CLI;` binding. The occurrence-location assertion (any `mindspec`/`codex` literal outside the array = failure, independent of textual match) closes BOTH failure modes I named: the strict-reading contradiction (no call-site literals remain) and the exact-match-bypass (`"mindspec panel create" + rawArg` now fails on location, not string equality). Item 2 (the round-4-critical one): fixed flags live only in the per-verb template; callers pass values only, so no arg position exists from which to inject `--sandbox`/`-s` — foreclosed MORE firmly than a blanket reject. The leading-dash guard, scoped to those values, still rejects a `--json` slug/target and correctly backstops `slug` (whose 110-inherited path-element validator does not itself reject a leading dash). Values are appended as argv-safe single tokens, never concatenated, so none can widen to a flag position; the template interpolation adds no new surface. No new hole.

findings.description: All four asks landed: destructured named verb constants from ALLOWED_CLI (L429-430); every buildCommand call passes one of the four identifiers, no call site retypes a command literal (R2 L490, codex L539, R5 L587-588); the identifier-count pin exists (Step 6, L618-627 — asserts exactly one `const [ID1,ID2,ID3,ID4] = ALLOWED_CLI;` binding, four wide, RHS literally ALLOWED_CLI). Crucially the added occurrence-location assertion (Step 6 + the 'Closing the exact-match bypass' block, L631-654) resolves both readings my round-2 RC flagged: strictly, there are now no call-site literals to trip 'no occurrence outside the array'; generously, an exact-literal-plus-concatenation bypass is caught by location, not string equality, so it is no longer vacuous. The 'by construction' claim is now honestly scoped to a static-text property the test actually verifies ('no mindspec-/codex-bearing literal can exist anywhere in the file outside the array declaration'). The only unaddressed sub-point is that the plan does not explicitly name the residual I mentioned (runtime `.replace`/string-surgery on a buildCommand result, or a telltale-free NL paraphrase in a prompt) — but that residual is outside what any static-text test can prove, the claim no longer overclaims it, and blocking a 9th-slot approval on adding one honesty sentence would be a thin nit the BRIEF warns against. Non-blocking.

The round-3 rework (L446-478) is sound. buildCommand assembles from a fixed per-verb template that alone supplies every fixed flag (`--spec`/`--target`/`--bead`/`--round`; codex `--sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check`); callers pass only user-derived values via `...args`; the leading-dash guard rejects any args element starting with `-`. (a) A flag-shaped value — a slug or target of `--json` — is STILL rejected, and this is the load-bearing backstop for `slug`, whose 110-inherited clean-single-path-element validator (`validatePanelSlug`: rejects empty/`.`/`..`/`/`/`\`/absolute/control-bytes) does NOT itself reject a leading dash. (b) A `--sandbox` override is foreclosed more firmly than under a blanket reject: since args carries no flags at all (flags live only in the template), there is no arg position from which to append a second `--sandbox`. (c) No new hole: `target` is appended as its own argv-safe single element 'never concatenated into a larger string' (L399-402), values fill value slots not flag positions, and any flag-shaped value is caught by the guard before interpolation — the template interpolation introduces no injection surface. `round` is a validated positive integer and never leads with `-`.

findings.area: no regression from the round-3 delta
findings.description: The `631c...`→`aec82bfd` reconciliation touches only buildCommand flag handling, the call-site identifier passing, the re-panel round-value note, and the target branch-name-safe grammar / e2e case 7b/7c additions. It does not disturb the ALLOWED_CLI exact-set, the codex read-only pin, the positive enumeration, the e2e reserialize/MISSING/quota ladders, or the doc-sync — carried-forward lenses remain intact.
